diagnostic_criteria.A: Five (or more) of the following symptoms present during the same 2-week period and represent a change from previous functioning; at least one of the symptoms is either (1) depressed mood or (2) loss of interest or pleasure.
diagnostic_criteria.B: The symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
diagnostic_criteria.C: The episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or to another medical condition.
diagnostic_criteria.D: The occurrence is not better explained by schizoaffective disorder, schizophrenia, delusional disorder, or other psychotic disorders.

symptoms.name: Depressed mood
symptoms.description: Nearly every day, subjectively reported or observed by others.

symptoms.name: Diminished interest
symptoms.description: Markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all or almost all activities.

symptoms.name: Weight/appetite change
symptoms.description: Significant weight loss when not dieting or weight gain, or decrease/increase in appetite.

symptoms.name: Insomnia or hypersomnia
symptoms.description: Nearly every day — difficulty falling or staying asleep, or sleeping excessively.

symptoms.name: Psychomotor agitation/retardation
symptoms.description: Observable psychomotor changes (restlessness or slowing).

symptoms.name: Fatigue or energy loss
symptoms.description: Fatigue or loss of energy nearly every day.

symptoms.name: Feelings of worthlessness
symptoms.description: Feelings of worthlessness or excessive/inappropriate guilt.

symptoms.name: Cognitive impairment
symptoms.description: Diminished ability to think, concentrate, or make decisions.

symptoms.name: Suicidal ideation
symptoms.description: Recurrent thoughts of death, recurrent suicidal ideation without a specific plan, or a suicide attempt.

diagnostic_criteria.A: A distinct period of abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood and abnormally increased activity or energy, lasting at least 1 week (or any duration if hospitalization required), present most of the day, nearly every day.
diagnostic_criteria.B: During the period of mood disturbance and increased energy or activity, three (or more) of the following are present (four if the mood is only irritable): inflated self-esteem or grandiosity; decreased need for sleep; more talkative than usual or pressure to keep talking; flight of ideas or subjective experience that thoughts are racing; distractibility; increase in goal-directed activity or psychomotor agitation; excessive involvement in activities with high potential for painful consequences.
diagnostic_criteria.C: The mood disturbance is sufficiently severe to cause marked impairment in social or occupational functioning or to necessitate hospitalization, or there are psychotic features.
diagnostic_criteria.D: The episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or to another medical condition.

symptoms.name: Elevated or irritable mood
symptoms.description: Abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.

symptoms.name: Grandiosity
symptoms.description: Inflated self-esteem or grandiosity.

symptoms.name: Decreased need for sleep
symptoms.description: Decreased need for sleep (feels rested after 3 hours).

symptoms.name: Pressured speech
symptoms.description: More talkative than usual or pressure to keep talking.

symptoms.name: Flight of ideas / racing thoughts
symptoms.description: Flight of ideas or subjective experience of racing thoughts.

symptoms.name: Distractibility
symptoms.description: Distractibility (attention too easily drawn to unimportant stimuli).

symptoms.name: Goal-directed activity / agitation
symptoms.description: Increase in goal-directed activity or psychomotor agitation.

symptoms.name: Risky/pleasurable activities
symptoms.description: Excessive involvement in activities with high potential for painful consequences.

name: Bipolar II Disorder

diagnostic_criteria.A: A distinct period of abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood and abnormally increased activity or energy (hypomanic episode) lasting at least 4 consecutive days.
diagnostic_criteria.B: Either the hypomanic episode or a major depressive episode must be a part of the current (or most recent) presentation.
diagnostic_criteria.C: There has never been a manic episode or mixed episode. The hypomanic episode causes clinically significant distress or impairment. The episode is not attributable to substance/medical condition.

symptoms: Same as Bipolar I manic criteria (hypomanic variant) + Major Depressive Episode criteria

diagnostic_criteria.A: Excessive anxiety and worry (apprehensive expectation), occurring more days than not for at least 6 months, about a number of events or activities.
diagnostic_criteria.B: The individual finds it difficult to control the worry.
diagnostic_criteria.C: The anxiety and worry are associated with three (or more) of the following six symptoms: restlessness or feeling keyed up; easily fatigued; difficulty concentrating; irritability; muscle tension; sleep disturbance.
diagnostic_criteria.D: The anxiety, worry, or physical symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment.
diagnostic_criteria.E: The disturbance is not attributable to substance/medical condition or another mental disorder.

symptoms.name: Restlessness or feeling on edge
symptoms.description: Feeling restless, keyed up, or on edge.

symptoms.name: Being easily fatigued
symptoms.description: Becoming easily fatigued.

symptoms.name: Difficulty concentrating
symptoms.description: Difficulty concentrating or mind going blank.

symptoms.name: Irritability
symptoms.description: Becoming easily irritated or annoyed.

symptoms.name: Muscle tension
symptoms.description: Muscle tension.

symptoms.name: Sleep disturbance
symptoms.description: Difficulty falling or staying asleep, or restless unsatisfying sleep.

diagnostic_criteria.additional: Two or more major depressive episodes in lifetime, with inter-episode period(s) in which criteria not met for a major depressive episode.

diagnostic_criteria.A: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks (abrupt surges of intense fear or discomfort) reaching a peak within minutes, accompanied by at least four of: palpitations; sweating; trembling; sensations of shortness of breath; chest pain; nausea or abdominal distress; dizziness; chills/heat; paresthesia; derealization; fear of losing control; fear of dying.
diagnostic_criteria.B: At least one of the attacks has been followed by 1 month or more of persistent concern about additional attacks or consequences, or significant maladaptive change in behavior.
diagnostic_criteria.C: The disturbance is not attributable to substance/medical condition or another mental disorder.

diagnostic_criteria.inattention: A persistent pattern of inattention and/or hyperactivity-impulsivity that interferes with functioning or development. Six (for older) or more (for ages 17+) symptoms from both the inattention and hyperactivity-impulsivity categories for at least 6 months.
inattention_items: Often fails to give close attention to details
Often does not seem to listen when spoken to directly
Often does not follow through on instructions
Often avoids/reluctant/grudgingly engages in tasks requiring sustained effort
Often easily distracted by extraneous stimuli
hyperactivity_items: Often 'on the go' acting as if driven

diagnostic_criteria.A: Persistent deficits in social communication and social interaction across multiple contexts, manifested by deficits in social-emotional reciprocity; nonverbal communicative behaviors; and developing/maintaining relationships.
diagnostic_criteria.B: Restricted, repetitive patterns of behavior, interests, or activity manifested by at least two types (stereotyped movements; insistence on sameness; highly restrictive/fixated interests; hyper/hyporeactivity to sensory input).
diagnostic_criteria.C: Symptoms present from early developmental period — though they may not become fully manifest until social demands exceed capacities.
diagnostic_criteria.D: Symptoms cause clinically significant impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of current functioning.
diagnostic_criteria.E: Disturbances not better explained by intellectual disability or global developmental delay.

diagnostic_criteria.A: Presence of obsessions, compulsions, or both. Obsessions: recurrent and persistent thoughts, urges, or images experienced as intrusive, unwanted, and causing marked anxiety; individual attempts to ignore/suppress or neutralize them. Compulsions: repetitive behaviors performed in response to an obsession or according to rigid rules.
diagnostic_criteria.B: The obsessions or compulsions are time-consuming (more than 1 hour per day) or cause clinically significant distress or impairment.
diagnostic_criteria.C: The obsessive-compulsive symptoms are not attributable to a substance/medical condition or another mental disorder.

diagnostic_criteria.description: Client does not meet criteria for any of the primary mental health disorders; scale scores within normal/expected ranges. May warrant routine periodic monitoring but no acute clinical intervention.
diagnostic_criteria.note: Coach/counselor should review with client to verify self-assessment accuracy and determine if further evaluation is warranted.

diagnostic_criteria.A: Excessive anxiety and worry more days than not for at least 6 months about a number of events or activities.
diagnostic_criteria.B: The individual finds it difficult to control the worry.
diagnostic_criteria.C: The anxiety and worry are associated with three or more of the following symptoms: restlessness; easily fatigued; difficulty concentrating; irritability; muscle tension; sleep disturbance.
diagnostic_criteria.E: Not attributable to substance/medical condition or another mental disorder.

diagnostic_criteria.A: A pervasive pattern of grandiosity (in fantasy or behavior), need for admiration, and lack of empathy, beginning by early adulthood.
grandiosity_criteria: Preoccupied with fantasies of unlimited success/power/brilliance
Belief that they are special and can only be understood by/associate with special people
Often envious of others or believes others are envious of them
Shows arrogant, haughty behaviors or attitudes
diagnostic_criteria.severity_note: Five or more of the nine criteria must be met for diagnosis.

diagnostic_criteria.A: A pervasive pattern of disregard for and violation of the rights of others, occurring since age 15, as indicated by at least three of the following: failure to conform to social norms with respect to lawful behaviors; deceitfulness; impulsivity or failure to plan ahead; irritability and aggressiveness; reckless disregard for safety of self or others; consistent irresponsibility; lack of remorse.
diagnostic_criteria.B: The individual must be at least 18 years old.
diagnostic_criteria.C: There is evidence of conduct disorder with onset before age 15.
